heading: National Biodefense Science Board and working groups
label: 247d–7g National Biodefense Science Board and working groups
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to .section 247d–7f of this title

children.heading: Establishment and function
children.label: 1 Establishment and function
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the National Biodefense Science Board (referred to in this section as the “Board”) to provide expert advice and guidance to the Secretary on scientific, technical and other matters of special interest to the Department of Health and Human Services regarding current and future chemical, biological, nuclear, and radiological agents, whether naturally occurring, accidental, or deliberate.

children.label: A
children.text: such Federal officials as the Secretary may determine are necessary to support the functions of the Board;

children.label: B
children.text: four individuals representing the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and device industries;

children.label: C
children.text: four individuals representing academia; and

children.label: i
children.text: one such member shall be a practicing healthcare professional;

children.label: ii
children.text: one such member shall be an individual from an organization representing healthcare consumers;

children.label: iii
children.text: one such member shall be an individual with pediatric subject matter expertise; and

children.label: iv
children.text: one such member shall be a State, tribal, territorial, or local public health official.

children.heading: Term of appointment
children.label: 3 Term of appointment
children.text: A member of the Board described in subparagraph (B), (C), or (D) of paragraph (2) shall serve for a term of 3 years, except that the Secretary may adjust the terms of the initial Board appointees in order to provide for a staggered term of appointment for all members.

children.heading: Consecutive appointments; maximum terms
children.label: 4 Consecutive appointments; maximum terms
children.text: A member may be appointed to serve not more than 3 terms on the Board and may serve not more than 2 consecutive terms.

children.label: A
children.text: advise the Secretary on current and future trends, challenges, and opportunities presented by advances in biological and life sciences, biotechnology, and genetic engineering with respect to threats posed by naturally occurring infectious diseases and chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear agents;

children.label: B
children.text: at the request of the Secretary, review and consider any information and findings received from the working groups established under subsection (b);

children.label: C
children.text: at the request of the Secretary, provide recommendations and findings for expanded, intensified, and coordinated biodefense research and development activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide any recommendation, finding, or report provided to the Secretary under this paragraph to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: Initial meeting
children.label: A Initial meeting
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Secretary shall hold the first meeting of the Board.December 19, 2006

children.heading: Subsequent meetings
children.label: B Subsequent meetings
children.text: The Board shall meet at the call of the Secretary, but in no case less than twice annually.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 7 Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy in the Board shall not affect its powers, but shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 8 Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint a chairperson from among the members of the Board.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: A Hearings
children.text: The Board may hold such hearings, sit and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the Board considers advisable to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Postal services
children.label: B Postal services
children.text: The Board may use the United States mails in the same manner and under the same conditions as other departments and agencies of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Employees of the Federal Government
children.label: A Employees of the Federal Government
children.text: A member of the Board that is an employee of the Federal Government may not receive additional pay, allowances, or benefits by reason of the member’s service on the Board.

children.heading: Other members
children.label: B Other members
children.text: A member of the Board that is not an employee of the Federal Government may be compensated at a rate not to exceed the daily equivalent of the annual rate of basic pay prescribed for level IV of the Executive Schedule under for each day (including travel time) during which the member is engaged in the actual performance of duties as a member of the Board.section 5315 of title 5

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: C Travel expenses
children.text: Each member of the Board shall receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in accordance with applicable provisions under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5.

children.heading: Detail of Government employees
children.label: D Detail of Government employees
children.text: Any Federal Government employee may be detailed to the Board with the approval for the contributing agency without reimbursement, and such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.
identify innovative research with the potential to be developed as a qualified countermeasure or a qualified pandemic or epidemic product;

identify accepted animal models for particular diseases and conditions associated with any biological, chemical, radiological, or nuclear agent, any toxin, or any potential pandemic infectious disease, and identify strategies to accelerate animal model and research tool development and validation; and

obtain advice regarding supporting and facilitating advanced research and development related to qualified countermeasures and qualified pandemic or epidemic products that are likely to be safe and effective with respect to children, pregnant women, and other vulnerable populations, and other issues regarding activities under this section that affect such populations.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: c Definitions
children.text: Any term that is defined in and that is used in this section shall have the same meaning in this section as such term is given in .section 247d–7e of this titlesection 247d–7e of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $1,000,000 to carry out this section for fiscal year 2007 and each fiscal year thereafter.
